user_comment: The Firpearl Underwire One Piece Swimsuit is made well, the fabric has enough stretch to comfortably get it on and off. The high cut causes a thinner look to the abdomen and the ruching is very slimming. The v-cut of the upper section is flattering. The lower section does not have extra fabric in place which would be preferred. The upper section is stitched in place so the inner fabric won't ride up in time. The high back is a nice change from the average racer-type suit. I would recommend it to anyone looking for a quality suit at a fair price. I have an uneven build and too big on the top to balance this suit out, I average between sizes 6 and 10 comfortably depending on the item. I ordered a size 8 in this suit in the hope it would fit both top and bottom. I wear a 32 H bra size and I was spilling over the upper section of this suit. It fit nicely overall and felt snug enough to stay put when swimming. Unfortunately, it just wasn't the right fitting suit for my shape. I recommend to anyone a little more "balanced" looking for a quality-made suit.

I'm not normally a fan of one-piece bathing suits, but I've put on some weight recently and was self-conscious about an upcoming trip to see my parents. I was drawn to this suit because of the "tummy control" feature, which-- to be honest-- ended up having nothing to do with why I liked it. The fabric feels incredibly sturdy, and the inner lining is black to ensure it doesn't become see-through. The coverage is modest enough for family outings yet casual enough to still look fun. The color, a beautifully rich almost-teal blue, is stunning in person. The straps are adjustable, but even so, I don't recommend this swimsuit for tall people. I found that even with my short torso, I had them mostly lengthened, with probably 3-4" to spare. For reference my current measurements are 5'2", 128 lbs, 35-29-39 and I ordered a size 8 that fits perfectly. As touched on earlier, the "tummy control" seems largely nonexistent beyond the scope of a normal stretchy suit, but the ruching definitely helps to disguise any bumps or rolls. I'm really impressed by how flattering the suit is while making me feel so comfortable.

Once, and for all , this is a pretty versatile, cute top that has a partial built in bra. Can flex all sorts of looks with this top. I just want to note, you may want to size up. Pros: -Beautiful bright pink, fuchsia color. -Unique Roche design patterning with criss cross. -the ties in the back are adjustable so you can make it tighter/tie in a cute way. (this is best for swimming as it will stay in place. Also, they have a nice stretch to them). -This can be worn as a swimsuit top as well as a casual fun summer shirt/crop top, as it has thicker tank like shoulder straps. I can see it dressed up with a fancy pair of jeans and a cute cropped jacket. (In example, I am wearing shiny black pants in my photos -almost a party look). -Can accessorize it to make it into more of a shirt! Dangling necklaces to chain belts would really stand out along with this top. -Works well as a swimsuit top/ material fit for pool or sea. The solid color is good to go with other solid colors or black and white. Cons: -Not exactly a push up. I didn’t feel that this top adds any lift or push up effect to the chest area. -Wiring under top is a bit tight/ not most comfortable. It seems like it only fits certain sizes/and could be uncomfortable for some. -Fit a bit tight all around- so cut in if bent. I got a size 8 (large I think) there was no sizing chart so wanted to include the following: I am a 36B (or C), and have 27/28 inch waist. I have a relatively longer torso (at 5’5 and a half), but this top was not very elongating. The tightness cuts into skin a bit. -So even though there is the wiring the small pads in there do not provide the most coverage. In addition to not much lift, they just sit there. I would probably use gels in addition if wearing this out and about as a casual top. Don’t get me wrong, there is still a good amount of coverage like typical swimsuits out there. -It maybe harder to match the type of pink that the top is to other pink items out there. You can tell it changes in lighting a bit. (I would recommend just white or black bottoms. I have pink shorts to pink jackets, but they were all different shades, sadly too off). Cute, but could be a bit more flexible in design and fit.

Please note that we modeled this with a special opaque dancer's bodystocking so you can see that this bikini has great potential. Admittedly, I have a hard time finding bikini sets that fit, but this one had a size chart that suggested it would be in the range and the lime green colour is really sharp. When we got it home, the fabric is good quality, and the sliders are sturdy and metal. The fit, however is where this goes a little sideways. While the top seems okay, it leaves me with absolutely no room for movement. To be sure, my measurements are 42-32-40 (equiv to a UK 36FF) and if I ordered a size up, I'd be swimming in the bottom because of the cut. The bottom is simply lovely but the top doesn't seem built for anyone more than a C-cup. 3 stars seems fair.

This would probably be a good top for women who are more endowed up top than myself lol. I'm really happy with this, and you can never go wrong with black bottoms because you can mix with any bikini top!

WOW!! This bathing suit far, far exceeded my expectations. For reference, I am 5’9” and currently weigh about 165. I am still carting around about 25 lbs of pregnancy weight, and putting a swimsuit on gives me anxiety. Seeing pictures of me in one gives me more! But I don’t hate how this looks on me, even if I’m not feeling myself still. The fit is INCREDIBLE. I got a size large based on the size chart and other reviews. I am SO used to one pieces fitting bad b/c of my height. I usually have to order up a couple sizes so that the suit doesn’t ride up or fit terribly in the chest area, and even then it’s so often a hard miss. This suit fits like a glove!! I’ll let you judge how much it helps w the tummy (and I have a mom pooch). It certainly doesn’t make the stomach look bigger anyway. I think the coverage is fantastic as well. There’s also enough support for the ladies. My single complaint is that it doesn’t come in fun print or colors!!! I’d seriously buy like 5 more of these if they had cute prints. But I do love the brown. It’s a very flattering color and will look nice w a good fake tan this summer. I am telling you, look no further for your PERFECT suit 🤩. Now let’s bring on some summer already!!!

I like the pattern on this swimsuit. Everyone has a different body type so it's not expected that every swimsuit will be a winning fit. I ordered this for my daughter who has an apple shape. She's larger on top, has a thick waist, but no hips, and a small rear. The swimsuit fit well to accommodate the top of her body but was too big on bottom. She stands 5'1". The straps are adjustable so the length of the swimsuit was good for her torso. She didn't care for the bows on the shoulders but they can easily be removed by cutting a couple of stitches. Ultimately she could not wear this swimsuit because the leg openings are high cut and they gaped open around the top of her thighs and crotch. If she sized down then the top would be too tight.

I ordered this swimsuit cover shirt and skirt set to have a fun clothing option while being on vacation. I love to go to the beach, but do not enjoy traversing the length of space between my door and the sand in just my swimsuit. This cover is quite classy and comfortable! While it is open weave, it allows for the feeling of being covered and somewhat obscured from sight. The material is soft and both pieces seem well made. This top is cropped with long bell sleeves, and honestly could be worn with shorts or jeans and a cami as an individual piece. However, the skirt I found to be a bit tight and constricting. I constantly have the problem that I need differing sizes between my top and bottom so I wasn't hugely surprised, but I had checked the size chart and was disappointed it didn't match up. It's not so tight as to be uncomfortable, I just wish it was looser. The skirt also has a small drawstring at the bottom so you can gather the skirt on one end to make it scrunch up a bit and show more leg. I would suggest ordering up, especially as you can see that even though their size chart says the waist is 30" for the medium, I'm getting 26" when I measured mine, which is way too small and so doesn't fit/drape like it shows on the listing model. I am 5'5, 135 lbs, 36D and I ordered the medium. The top is loose and fits me well, but the bottom is tight and form fitting, so consider that when purchasing. Overall, it's cute, comfortable, and a nice matching set, and I would recommend it to others looking for a cute beach cover set!

SEE BELOW FOR WHY I DROPPED MY RATING IN LESS THAN 1 year First I have learned to always read reviews online, especially with clothing, suits,& bras. For two main reasons how it fits others with a similar body type and how the sizing runs. That said I am 5’1” 138lbs and my bra size is 32K (YES K! 😳) and I ordered a Large this suit fits very nice and of course my hubby loves it! It holds the girls in (although I did think the breast portion was moveable it is not so I showed two pics one with them more separated). I will say one person answered my question and I took their advice n went with what my body type is rather than bra size. There is a small amount of side boob as I call it but not to where there’s spillage. If I would have gone bigger the back would have been too large! UPDATED REVIEW! RATING DROPPED from 5 to 2! In less than one year this swim suit has lost ALL elasticity in the breasts and belly and is all see thru in the netted areas!!! I noticed this over time using it frequently while going into a hot tub. I also noticed black dots but not dirt when I would get in. Today I wiped my suit on my hand and sure enough it is LITERALLY FALLING APART. It will ruin our filter so on to a better quality suit. I may have expected a little more life than I should have given the price, but lesson learned. I hope this helps someone! Last two pics show how loose and the black stuff rubbing off into my hand!

I love old movies, and I especially like the sportswear styling of the 1940s. Because I appreciate retro style, I was drawn to the charming polka dot pattern and the overall design of the Yonique high-waisted bikini swimsuit. The nylon/spandex material is soft and comfortable, and the tummy control feature is subtle, which is great for hot weather. The top's clasp and neck ties provide a secure fit, and the removable pads are a nice touch, although I prefer to go without them. The back clasp has three options, but it is a little hard to hook. The skirt is flouncy and attached to a bikini bottom that ensures coverage. The modesty factor goes hand in hand with ‘40s retro! However, there were a couple of drawbacks that prevented me from fully embracing this swimsuit. Despite ordering a size large to accommodate my 5'8" height and 150lbs weight, the skirt was surprisingly long, making it impractical for swimming. It's more suited for lounging by the pool or tennis court with an adult beverage. Additionally, the ruching on the side, intended to adjust the length, proved fragile as one of the strings broke the second I tried to adjust it. This makes it essentially a skirt. Overall, while I appreciate the aesthetic and will still wear it for certain occasions, it's more of an outfit than a functional bathing suit due to the skirt's length and the fragility of the ruching strings.

First thing to note, there were no size tags on the garment to indicate what size it was. I ordered a large, but there is no telling if that is the actual size I received. I wanted to love this suit and knew it was a gamble because it had structured cups and I am rather busty (32L), but the sizing on this just no way would work. The suit itself is very beautiful, the fabric is not see through even though it is white and it feels soft and smooth. The images on it are clear and the constructions is of good quality. It feels like the put the same size cups on a size large as they would have on the small. I posted some photos of one of my actual bra's in comparison to the cups on this suit. They suits cups don't even cover a 1/4 of my bra cups. When I put it on it was so ridiculous looking. Either the wires sat on top of my boobs in the center like a weird on top pastie with the curves of my breasts on the bottom and sides or I put the wire under my boobs and the cups disappread because my boobs overlapped them and of course I was completely exposed. The torso of the suit is also quite long, I am short (5') but I had about 5 inches of extra fabric hanging in the crotch. It is also a very high leg and cheeky cut. The last weird sizing is the matching sarong. It is so pretty, but It also appears to be a one size and it is very small. When I tied it around my waist (33") the sides did not meet and had about an 8 inch gap between the two. my whole crotch, thighs or a butt cheek would be hangin out deepening on where I put the meeting point. I would suggest this suit for a tall, small chested, thin woman. It did not work on a short curvy girl at all.

This is an awesome swimsuit for anyone concerned with sun protection. I ordered this for long days in and on the water, and I couldn't be happier with the design. It isn't apparent from the description photos, but this is truly a one piece, with the shorts attached (it also has a built in bra with removable pads). It isn't what I had expected, but I like it for water sports. Unlike a lot of one piece swimsuits, this one is actually easy to put on and take back off without dislocating a shoulder; I normally shy away from front zippers, but this one feels secure. The material is comfortably stretchy and supportive, and overall feels and looks high quality. I'm a middle aged woman who has recently gained a lot of weight, but I think this swimsuit is still flattering. For reference, I am 5 foot 7, weigh 170 pounds, have a 36C bust with narrow shoulders, a short torso as mentioned, and a wide but flat derriere, carry most of my weight in the middle, and wear a US women's size 10 or 30 pants (medium size pants and joggers at Old Navy and H&M), or a woman's 10 or 12 in dresses. I ordered this swimsuit in the "large" size, and the fit is perfect (see my pics). This is a comfortable, high quality swimsuit that offers great sun protection, and I would not hesitate to order again from this brand.

It’s so hard to find a swimsuit that is cute AND holds everything in place when you’re a mom of 3, especially when you have some extra jiggliness from losing a little bit of weight. Buying swimsuits online makes it even more difficult. So I personally appreciate seeing actual pictures of real people wearing items when trying to make decisions. That being said I’m 5’4”, 150 lbs, 34D, with a short waisted hourglass figure, wear size 10 in jeans, and generally a medium or sometimes large depending on the brand or style, but I tend to order swimsuits in a large. I really love this swimsuit! I ordered it in a large and it fits great. I like that the style is cute and a little different with the tummy cut out, but not too revealing. It’s a slate blue with a white top and black stripes. The suit itself is comfortable and I’m confident it will stay in place swimming, kayaking, and chasing my kids. It’s made of a nice quality fabric that is not thin or see through. It doesn’t suck you in per say, but it does feel a little supportive and the runching helps hide mommy tummy. The entire suit is lined, front and back. The top inserts are not removable and take up the whole busy area of the top. They are not awkwardly shaped or weird looking and you can’t see them through the fabric. The straps are adjustable and although there is no underwire, you are able to tie the back and help tighten the underbust area to provide a bit of support. Delivery was supposed to take a month (it came from China), but it actually arrived in about 2 weeks. I do really like this swimsuit and anticipate wearing it frequently on our upcoming cruise and into the summer. If you are thinking about getting this one I would highly recommend it because it’s a great quality swimsuit.

I really love this swimsuit! I ordered it in a large and it fits great. I like that the style is cute and a little different with the tummy cut out, but not too revealing. It’s a slate blue with a white top and black stripes. The suit itself is comfortable and I’m confident it will stay in place swimming, kayaking, and chasing my kids. It’s made of a nice quality fabric that is not thin or see through. It doesn’t suck you in per say, but it does feel a little supportive and the runching helps hide mommy tummy. The entire suit is lined, front and back. The top inserts are not removable and take up the whole busy area of the top. They are not awkwardly shaped or weird looking and you can’t see them through the fabric. The straps are adjustable and although there is no underwire, you are able to tie the back and help tighten the underbust area to provide a bit of support. Delivery was supposed to take a month (it came from China), but it actually arrived in about 2 weeks. I do really like this swimsuit and anticipate wearing it frequently on our upcoming cruise and into the summer. If you are thinking about getting this one I would highly recommend it because it’s a great quality swimsuit.

Omg, I have such a hard time finding a suit that is long enough for my torso, doesn’t flatten my already flat chest, and hides my belly without making it look like it’s squeezing the life out of me. The adjustable straps are wonderful and make my feel secure in my chest but not suctioned. The bottom is full coverage and wonderful! The belly area is super cute and hides imperfections. I am 160lbs 5’6” and carry my weight in my hips/thighs. The leg holes are very well fitted. I ordered a large and overall it fits well! Nothing is too big/small which I was worried about with having a small chest because usually the top is way too big and the bottoms fit. I will probably order more colors in this because I love it so much!

Anddd, when I sit down, I can pull up the piece of fabric by the hole & cover my tummy roll 😅 I will be getting more in other colors 👏🏻🖤

I am using this high quality one piece, boyshort legs suit for aquatic therapy. Specifically the type of therapy where you walk/run on a treadmill in pool water. I also do calesthetic exercises i.e. side leg lifts, squats, full body dips and kicks. I only tell you all this just you can see how rigorous a workout this suit can handle. The straps don’t move, the legs do not roll up and the chest area needs no adjustment. Although the legs look short on the suit by itself, when you put the suit on they actually are on my body 4 inches down from the top of my inner thigh. I have chosen to remove the extra padding for the chest area which the suit arrived with. I am apple shaped, and my legs are skinny by comparison to my waist. My waist does not curve in and consider myself straight up and down. The suit was easy enough to put on considering I have a bum right shoulder, osteoarthritis and a bad back. Ergo all the therapy. In getting out of the glass water tank I can simply pull the good arm out first and then roll the suit down and off! So easy! Btw I am 63 years old and removing a wet suit was key to my happiness with a suit. If you are a young lady in any shape this would be a really pretty, flattering suit to go to the beach or pool. Don’t let me forget for those who will be using this in the sun the material is UPF 50+. The photos I don’t believe do the suit justice. It is much thicker than it appears and the colors are more vibrant. I’ve attached a photo of the navy and I hope you can see the pretty blues. I would highly recommend.

My wife and I picked this beautifully colored swimsuit together. The boyleg style offers a little more coverage, so you automatically feel more at ease about your appearance. The sizing seemed small at first, but the material freely stretches to conform to your body. Even at a 36DDD, the swimsuit held up great and looked good while doing it. It was not too tight at all. We gave it a perfect 5-star rating overall, but we were a little disappointed at how unflattering the midsection was. Unless you're built like a supermodel, every detail of your midsection will be very visible. I think some tummy support bands would have gone a long way to make this swimsuit even more perfect. At just south of $30, this is a great buy. We highly recommend it.

To start off, I am 5'4" and I weigh around 155lbs - I got a large. I am not a super thin person but I'm also not overly curvy which may be important for optimal flattery. There's a few things I really like about this suit and a few I don't so I'll just do pros and cons. Pros: * The fabric is thick! There's two layers, one is the outer, tighter fabric. The inside is a softer, more meshy fabric that makes it a lot more comfortable. * I wore this thing twice in all and both times I had zero issues with wearing it for a long time. With a of of strappy suits you have an uncomfortable tightness or painful pulling on the strings (especially as a not so thin person) but I just didn't have that with this one. * Despite the fabric being thick and strong, it still dried very quickly after being in the sun. I hate soggy suits so this was a major win for me. * The tummy control it real. Obviously its just fabric and can only do so much, but it really is a relief not to have to worry about my mommy tummy saying hi to everyone too. * The back straps are really cute and come down pretty far so tan lines aren't as quirky. Cons: * This is on me but I did NOT like the color. I ordered the 'Cream Coffee' one thinking it would be darker than my skin (because I'm really pale) but its a lot lighter than the photos and ended up being more like a skin suit for me. * I love the idea of the retro, high hip cutout but they translated the thickness all the way around leaving a LOT of cheeky exposure. Had I been curvier, it may have been okay but with this color and the sort of bagginess it just looked bad. * When I first got the suit I wore it as is to the beach and washed it when I got home. After the wash I noticed that the stitching on the strap near the front is already coming unraveled. This actually alarms me because you don't want a tight, body control type item to have weak hems. Overall, I do like this suit. I ended up making some adjustments so I feel confident keeping it in my wardrobe. I got a little mesh skirt for when I'm not in the water and I also just dyed the suit an entirely different color. I wish I had just gotten a darker color to begin with, I may have liked it more right from the start as the booty area looks infinitely better when it doesn't match my flesh. I'm going to have to re-sew all of the hems though because the last thing I want is a wardrobe malfunction at the beach.

Only negatives I could find with it is the shoulder straps aren’t adjustable and they are quite tight, but that’s a good thing considering you can’t make them tighter and I’d prefer my shoulder straps don’t slip off mid swim 😂 just take that part into account when picking a size! And the chest padding is very bumpy, it’s possibly just wrinkled up because of how it’s packaged but I’m going to hand wash them while my suit is in the wash and see if that helps it. Honestly with the thickness of the material, might be able to get away with not using the padding at all but I haven’t tested that yet!

Only downsides are: the clasp in the back is plastic and I've always had these break on suits so I'm going to do what others did and replace it with a metal key-ring. I am 5'4 almost 150 lb. My chest size is 34d. I got this suit in a size large and it fits perfectly. I recently gained about 30 lbs and am very self-conscious about how I look. I do think that this bathing suit is very flattering. The top has padding that can be removed. The bottoms are a little narrowly cut in the back. So it's not a full coverage bottom. The cover up is probably my favorite part. It is a four-way stretch sheer fabric that is very comfortable. It hasn't elastic waist. It has channels on each side of the hip with ties that can be adjusted either high or low. That is probably my favorite part. I love this suit and I think it is a great value for the money. I highly recommend.

The first time I ordered it in a medium and the top was too small, the cups weren’t wide enough. However, the bottoms mostly fit. Then this time, I ordered this set in a large and the top fits better…still not perfect. However, the bottoms are slightly too large and baggy in the crotch. I feel like I’m pretty evenly proportioned, so I don’t know why I require two different sizes. I think the fit is off with this one. Also, there is some puckering on the shell bra top. I will say though that I love the aqua color. While it isn’t fluorescent like the listing images, it is still a nice bright aqua that makes my skin look tan.

Overall a really nice style with good back coverage, very high quality material, but not at all shaping/compressive, and lacking in some details. I returned them all because I found better.

Y’all. This suit. BUY IT. I am curvy: 175 pounds, short, small-medium chested, large booty and hips, non-flat stomach. Specifically: 5’1”, bust 40 (b cup), waist 33, hips 45. I ordered a large in this suit. I literally have avoided the pools in past summers because I always got so frustrated at the limited bathing suit options for me in the stores...Not quite plus size, but not thin. Everything I would try on was either too big, or made me look like a muffin popping out of its pan. After sweating my butt off under the sun last week without a swimsuit, I went online in hopes of finding something that was at least passable. I chose this suit because of all the raving reviews from curvy ladies like myself; They looked BANGIN! Ordered this suit and it was at my front door in LESS than 24 hours! Tried it on, initial observation was that it came on like a glove; No pulling or forcing it to fit. Looked in the mirror expecting to have to return it, my mouth literally dropped. NO insanely obvious stomach pooch, my upper body looked thin and elongated because of the cut, and no squeezing at my thighs or booty. The perfect amount of cheeky in the back; A little peak, but didn’t get sucked up/needing constant adjustment. A beautiful scoop back that hid all my uncomfortable back rolls. A SEXY cut in the front that both contained and revealed just enough. This is the best bathing suit I’ve ever owned and I might buy it in more colors because I’m so pleased with the fit. I AM GOING TO THE POOL THIS YEAR!

YES, finally! A two-piece bathing suit that looks good even on a more mature mom-body! For reference, I wear a size 10/12 usually, or size Medium US size, weigh 175-lbs give or take a few, have a 36C chest and size 32-34" waist. I got the size Large, my usual European size, and it fits perfectly! The very first thing I did was remove the pads in the top, I never use those. The fabric is plenty thick enough without them. My chest fits well and I am not afraid of spilling out and shocking the world. The bottoms hug me and give full coverage but don't look like granny-anything. I am really self-conscious in bathing suits and am trying to get out of that mind-set and start wearing something more practical for kayaking and backpacking. A two-piece swimsuit is ideal and easy to get back into wet. I love the flowers and colors. The suit reminds me of the pretty china dishes I have always loved, or Holland. My husdand thought it was really nice I was wearing something that has color (I usually go for all black) and not draping myself so fully. The feels good swimming laps, or wearing under other clothes so you are ready to take a dip in a lake. I am so happy I made the decision to try it!

The quality of this material is excellent. It’s stretchy, yet fits securely. I enjoy the style. The swimsuit is very comfortable to wear while remaining sheek at the same time. I also really love the color. As shown in the photos, I am wearing the navy blue. There’s an adjustable strap that comes along with the suit, you can attach to the hooks on the front and the strap fits securely around the back of your neck. Or you can wear the suit strapless. I find it to be supportive both with and without the strap. My nieces love to swim, one still uses floaties and when tired she often latches on to me. It helps to have a suit that doesn’t fall down and fits securely for when you are swimming with a toddler! I’m wearing this bikini in size large as shown in the photos. I am a 32DDD in bra size, so majority of the tops I wear are a large. In bottoms I am normally a size 4 or usually wearing a medium. My weight is 125 lbs and I’m 5’4” in height. As for my measurements (in inches) using a measuring tape, my bust = 37-38 in, hip = 37-38 in, and waist = 28 in. I am a bit larger in my upper body (having a larger bust) in proportion to my lower body, so shopping swimwear can get to be a bit tricky. I own several of the two-piece bikinis by Relleciga and size large has always fit perfectly for my tops *and bottoms*. I went with my instinct again and ordered size large in this one-piece, and it fit how I expected! The bottom of the suit fits snug but not loose. The upper part isn’t tight and does have some breathing room. It’s great quality, stretchy fabric. I expect it will last a long time as with my other Relleciga swimwear.

I HAD to get this beautiful pink rouched bathing suit! Pink is one of my favorite colors and the only other bathing suits I have are black. Granted black is slimming but who wants to wear black in summer? It does come in other colors and patterns which are very nice. It is made of 80% Polyamide and 20% Spandex so it is a bit stretchy. It is recommended to hand wash in cold water and line dry. It also is nicely lined and the construction is quality. According to their size chart a Large is: 36D or DD and 38B or C, Waist is: 31.5-33.5”, and Hips are: 40.5-42.5”. My bust isn’t quite that big at only 36B, my waist is 35-36”, and my hips are 43”. I went mostly by the waist and hip measurements to get the best size. I just love the style of this swim suit with it’s rouching which won’t get rid of a tummy, but can distract from or minimize it. The leg openings don’t go too high up which is great. But the top part is better for busty girls. It’s more flattering. I show both with and without straps, and personally I think(because my bust isn’t that big), that I look better with straps. Busty girls would look better without(my opinion). Since I am tall at 5’9”, and have a long torso, this fits a bit different than someone with a normal torso. It does fit a long torso but everything is stretching more. The back top part comes just under your shoulder blades, and the backside bottom part is modest enough for most women. I also just noticed in their video that the model fastened the straps together at the back of her neck, so that is a third way you can wear the straps. The price of this swim suit is very reasonable for such a good looking and quality piece. All in all, highly recommended!

I may have a coverup problem—I keep ordering them! This one from Ekouaer is wonderful! the tropical pattern and bright colors are vibrant and fun. This button-down, collared coverup is loose and comfortable. ordered size large and at 5’8” and 150lbs, this is definitely tunic length. I love the versatility—beautiful beach cover in the summer and shirt dress with leggings in the off-season. This coverup is very well constructed. The buttons and buttonholes are tight with no fraying, and the seams are sewn tightly with no loose threads. Looking for a stylish and practical cover-up for your next beach trip? Look no further than Ekouaer's Long Roll-Sleeve Beach Shirt! Here's my honest review after taking it for a spin on the shore. Pros: Comfy and Breathable: The fabric is lightweight and soft, perfect for hot days at the beach. It drapes nicely and allows for good air circulation, keeping you cool and comfortable. Sun Protection: The long sleeves offer a welcome layer of protection from the sun's harmful rays. This is especially great if you're prone to sunburn or want to avoid overexposure. The sleeves can be rolled up for a more relaxed look when desired. Versatile Style: This cover-up can be worn in multiple ways. Wear it loose and flowing for a casual beach look, or tie the sleeves in a knot for a more cinched silhouette. You can even button it up and wear it as a breezy beach shirt. Decent Length: The length hits around the mid-thighs on me (at 5'6"), which provides good coverage while still allowing for easy movement. Affordable Price: This cover-up is priced very competitively, making it a great value for the quality. Cons: Can be Sheer: The fabric is on the thinner side, so it can be a bit see-through, especially in lighter colors. If you're looking for maximum opacity, you might want to choose a darker color or wear a tankini or swimsuit underneath. Limited Sizing: While Ekouaer offers a range of sizes, some reviewers mentioned that the sizing can run a bit small, so you might want to consider sizing up for a looser fit. Overall: Ekouaer's Long Roll-Sleeve Beach Shirt is a great choice for anyone who wants a stylish and comfortable cover-up that also offers sun protection. The breathable fabric, versatile design, and affordable price make it a winner in my book. Just keep in mind the potential for sheerness and consider sizing up if needed. Rating: 4.5 out of 5 stars I hope this review helps you decide if Ekouaer's Long Roll-Sleeve Beach Cover-Up is the right pick for you!

Wear it loose and flowing for a casual beach look, or tie the sleeves in a knot for a more cinched silhouette. You can even button it up and wear it as a breezy beach shirt. Decent Length: The length hits around the mid-thighs on me (at 5'6"), which provides good coverage while still allowing for easy movement. Affordable Price: This cover-up is priced very competitively, making it a great value for the quality. Cons: Can be Sheer: The fabric is on the thinner side, so it can be a bit see-through, especially in lighter colors. If you're looking for maximum opacity, you might want to choose a darker color or wear a tankini or swimsuit underneath. Limited Sizing: While Ekouaer offers a range of sizes, some reviewers mentioned that the sizing can run a bit small, so you might want to consider sizing up for a looser fit. Overall: Ekouaer's Long Roll-Sleeve Beach Shirt is a great choice for anyone who wants a stylish and comfortable cover-up that also offers sun protection. The breathable fabric, versatile design, and affordable price make it a winner in my book. Just keep in mind the potential for sheerness and consider sizing up if needed. Rating: 4.5 out of 5 stars I hope this review helps you decide if Ekouaer's Long Roll-Sleeve Beach Cover-Up is the right pick for you!

I like this fun and funky pattern for a beach coverup. I was expecting it to be a more gauzy/chiffon type fabric, but it’s a stretchy polyester shirt fabric. It is in fact quite comfortable and lightweight, but it’s not opaque. The length is quite long. For me at 5’7” the front comes below my mid-thigh and the back almost reaches the back of my knee. I typically am a size XL, but I ordered a L in this one, and I’m glad I did. It fits really nicely. I’d assume an XL would be pretty oversized for me. So you may consider sizing down one for a slightly more fitted feel. A couple of disappointing factors for me are 1) the fabric wrinkles easily. For packing, it will require ironing or steaming upon arriving. 2) there is no tab for rolling up the sleeves. It is long-sleeved, but I was expecting to have a button and tab to roll up the sleeves, as I probably won’t plan to wear it rolled down. Neither are huge factors, but I did decide to take one star off my review as a result! Overall it’s a nice top, and appears to be good quality.

Also, I always do better in petites because of my height and the short distance between the top of my shoulder and top of my bust. I purchased a large in this suit. This swimsuit is really flattering because the top is so adjustable. I had to adjust the straps about 75% of the way up and also make sure the back tie was snug to feel nicely supported up top, but I can say for sure that I feel secure in this. The fabric is thick and has nice elasticity without being loosey goosey, and although there is no compression panel in the front it is still snug enough to feel good. Hope this helps someone else - I will be keeping this and can’t wait to wear it!

I was very apprehensive when I pulled this top out of the package... even in a size large, it looked pretty tiny, and I have a 32G chest to cover. 😳 Pretty shocking to pull it on, and it fit!! A little bit of underboob, but actually I didn't mind how it looked. Kinda sexy IMO. The fabric stretched just enough to cover, but was still sturdy enough to support... a combo that's actually really difficult to find. Tops in letter sizes (as opposed to bra sizing) are usually a no go for me, since getting enough cup/coverage almost always means the band is too big and I fall out the bottom. I removed the cup padding as it gave a weird shape. It was also soooo comfortable, with no clips or clasps or any other hardware to dig in to your skin. I didn't care for the bottoms, but that was fine since I was only interested in the top anyway (to match a different pair of bottoms I already had). I'm usually a M in bottoms, but even in the L I thought these were very unflattering on me. I like a high waist but these weren't quite high enough, just hit in a weird spot. The leg holes also cut in a little bit on me, instead of just laying smooth. Quality-wise, it's holding up great so far! I wore it swimming in salt water and pool water, got sunscreen all over it I'm sure, and washed it when I got home. Still looks great. If you're in the small band/large cup category like me, give this bikini a shot!

This is such a cute, cute swimsuit. Its such a great cut and the bi-color pink colors are awesome. Some reviews I had read were nightmares with the color transferring all over other garments or in the wash. We didn't have quite that same experience, but it did have some odd color transfer after a rinse and hang dry. I ended up hand washing the two separate colors by themselves (so that took some effort) and laying out to dry and that seemed to do the trick much better. The good thing is that doing the hand wash, it was good as new and all the dye it had seemed to pick up was cleared out.This suit also has the sewn in pads, which is a little bit of a bummer, but thankfully they fit my wife really well and she is happy with how they are - and she always uses pads, so its not like she was going to take them out unless it was to replace with other ones.As for how the suit fits my wife, she loves how it fits and how she looks in it. The stomach of the suit holds her in (a mom of two) and she appreciates the flattening and flattering that it does for her. The upper stomach cutout is a cute feature and works so well with the bi-color.Be cautious for how you care for the suit. We need to wash it again sometime and see if it still takes extra care for keeping the color bright, but for her wearing it on our cruise, she really loved it!

If you have an injured or arthritic shoulder you might not tolerate such acrobatics. The zipper is a bit short IMO. I wasn't expecting much support from the sewn in bra, but it worked amazingly well in the water and outside of the pool. I ordered size L for my 5'5" 165lb frame with 36C bust and this fit perfectly. The girls had excellent support as they fit into the cups as they would normally. I felt the half-band under my breasts as it should have been anatomically. The girls were supported in the pool while actively swimming. However, if you have a bust that's larger than mine then the cups might be too small for you. Size C fits to the max. As for the fabric, it's fantastic. It's thick, non see through, doesn't become sheer in the pool, stretches with resistance, lifts my belly and butt, so I actually look a lot slimmer than I am. I love the long sleeves because they have holes for the thumb. And this dress has POCKETS! I loved it so much, even though it's hard to get out of, that's my only negative about this garment. I'll be getting another color.

I think suntan lotion would make it off color, too. I do not recommend this top unless you only plan to use it for a short time or only in fresh or salt water. P.S.- pics are from before chlorine exposure

I am really in love with this BUTYKENNI 2pc Bikini! The floral print is so beautiful and bright!! The contrasting white and red zig zag like edging detail is also a cool touch! I ordered this bathing suit in a Large. Now, I usually wear a small but I always check the product sizing chart and go with what size it suggests. I was a little worried that it would be too big, but it totally fits me great...so I would recommend ordering up a size or two and definitely follow the sizing chart!!! The quality of this bikini is very good and I find it comfortable to wear. The top has cute double straps that are adjustable and has very thin removable padding. I cannot speak too much on the support quality of the top, well because I really don't have much there to support, lol! With that being said, I will definitely be replacing the not-so-great padding with some that I have which are bigger & better! The only thing that I am not happy about is the crappy clasp on the back. I have noticed that this type of cheap closure is being used more often now, but I just cannot seem to get used to it and I am not a fan. I still like the old school style of the bikini top hook that easily goes into an opening in the bathing suit material! The bottom of this bikini is not for the shy, as it is does have a higher cut and is a skimpier style bottom. I would say that it is in-between a brazilian and a cheeky bottom. It provides enough bottom coverage for me whether I am just laying out or taking a quick dip! So, I also like that it has the same double side straps that match the bikini top, it definitely pulls the whole look together! Honestly, I would probably opt for a more conservative "mom" bottom when out and about in public with my teenage sons! I don't want to embarrass them, well at least with not what I am wearing, nor do I ever want to portray that I am trying to act or look any younger than I am. But, at exactly twelve days away from turning 43 🥳...I am not afraid to say that I do love how this bikini looks on me and that I feel quite cute & sexy when wearing it! Plus, it is nice to sometimes catch my hubby of 16 yrs still looking at me 👀...like that! Good old fashioned confidence boost right there ladies, even if you think you don't need it!!! I 💯 percent recommend this product, just remember to double check the sizing chart before you go ahead and order your "usual" size, and trust that it will be the right/best fit for you, because it worked out well for me even though I second guessed it after I ordered this in a size Large!!!! I will be sporting this two-piece bathing suit all summer long and yeah, I think that you should too!! Oh, and I added my measurements, and etc. below for your reference if ya'll need it. 💕 height: 5'0" weight: 115 lbs. bust: 36A waist: 28 inches hip: 39 inches thigh: 22 inches

I absolutely love this bikini. It is super stylish and it fits perfectly for a day lounging by the pool. I have a large chest (32F) and small hips (33.5") and butt, so I usually have a hard time getting bikini sets that fit. This bikini hits the mark reasonably well. I bought the size large and, when it's tied at it's tightest, fits my hips relatively tightly. I don't have to worry about them falling down, but probably wouldn't want to do any diving, haha. The top fits better than I though too. With the padding it was way too small, but I took the padding out and it fits pretty well everywhere, other than a little spillage on my side boob. It would be a great sunbathing bikini the way it fits right now, but I would be a little worried about nip slips if I were to do any water sports it it. For this reason I contacted the seller on the off-chance they could work with me with the sizing issues, and they were so !! The bikini seems to be really well made and feels thick, and I absolutely love the style. I definitely recommend this and the company. A+ all around.

I am impressed by the design and quality of this suit! I ordered the Turquoise color in size Large (US 10). - Color - nice, rich deep color. - Material and stitching feel to be a good quality, good weight for bathing suite materials and do not feel see-through. The stitching looks solid, so no concerns there. - True Fit. According to chart, I was between a Medium and Large but sized up because I always have an issue with the suit top being too small. Their size chart: 38 Bust 32 Waist 40 Hips 36C-36D/38A-38B Cups Me: 37/38 Bust 30 Waist 37 Hips 36C/38B Cups I'm glad I went with the size up top to guide my size decision. Straps/Top - Overall, this suit is nice for those who are larger up top. The straps are thick on top of shoulders to support chest. They are adjustable, always nice, since I am a bit bigger in shoulders. The cinch/tie in the middle allows for a bit of customization on size and how conservative you'd like to be. Bottoms - I feel these fit a bit smaller than in the size chart. According the chart, a Medium was my size but I'm glad I ordered a Large as I think a smaller size would have been a little too tight for my liking. Durability - I will update this review if anything negative comes up. Otherwise, you can assume all is sufficient, I am still happy with it and would recommend. :)

I had a C-section a few years ago and still have a significant belly-hangover (apron belly, FUPA, call it what you will) to remind me. I didn't put on a swimsuit for nearly 3 years, but couldn't avoid it any longer. I tried on AT LEAST 30 (if not more) different "tummy control" swimsuits before ordering this one... I kept a couple others, but they're all halter neckline which apparently is no longer comfortable for me, so I wanted something with regular straps. I would have saved so much time and agony had I started with this one! It's hands down the most flattering and comfortable option. I'm going to order a 2nd color. It doesn't feel like the tight/compressing tummy control that some suits have, but it does SOMETHING. The cutout in the back is also nicely positioned to conceal some flank back flab. I'm 5'7, 185 lbs, 38DD/36DDD, usually wear size Large, but sometimes XL or Medium, depending on the brand. I got this suit in a large and love it. That said I DO wish it had more chest coverage / support... my little one is still learning to swim and constantly grabbing at me, so unfortunately this may not be the right suit for more active swim days. The first color I ordered (worn in the pics) is "Turquoise Green"... I wanted green, so I'm very glad it looks more hunter green than teal blue. Does it completely transform my body or do any favors to the cellulite on my thighs? No, it does not. But I did just enjoy swimming in a busy hotel pool with without being completely miserable, so it wins.

This is a nice swimsuit top but has a few issues for me. Good construction, stitching is nice, seams are sewn straight and no loose thread tails. Outer fabric 80% nylon/ 20% spandex is excellent with wonderful stretch and recovery. Purple color is vibrant and fully saturated. Bra is stiff because polyester liner has little stretch while outer has good stretch. Doesn’t make sense to line with inferior material. Uni-boob, no shape to the cups without the inserts, just flat material that tends to flatten and mash me. Hook is swimming suit style, large 1 inch hook with 3 different hook points though only the last one works well. The other two positions cause a large flap pf fabric under the band that is uncomfortable. Adjustable straps are narrow and have small hardware that is difficult to adjust as the straps want to roll in the buckle. Not sure why there are a stack of 6 tags but they constantly stick out from under the strap. Removable cups are thin but still show thru fabric, I removed them. Bra band was small on me, even on the last hook, it was still tight. My normal size is 38 B. For reference I am 40/36/39, 6’ tall and 187 pounds

I noticed most reviews did not leave photos of what the suit looks like on. I decided to be brave and give a very realistic review. I think it’s a good quality item for the price.

So, if you are tall and long torso the cups may not go high enough for you. Short, average or slightly long torso should be fine.

Love it! I don't usually go for one pieces, but I am constantly losing one piece to my matching sets! Consistently leaving me with a useless, mismatched mess! So I figured a one piece would be more convenient and decided to try one out. I love this one! I was really skeptical, as someone whose weight fluctuates a lot and on top of that I bloat pretty bad too (GI issues), that this could remain comfortable. So I decided to try it on after eating a big meal and drinking some water to see how it would hold up and feel if I decided to eat a good meal at a party or if I got bloated while I was out in it and to my surprise, it felt great! It stretched with me and didn't feel too restricting or uncomfy like the one piece swimsuits from my childhood did. Cons: I wish there was a little more chest support. The pads are great for covering up better, but the support isn't really there. Just the compression of the bathing suit material. Not a huge issue for me as a size 38D, but if you had a larger chest and planned on doing anything sporty in it, it could be less comfortable. Length could be an issue for taller people. I'm 5'3 and this was just the perfect length for me, I'd imagine this length could begin to be problematic for folks over 5'7.

This arrived quickly. I was sent the wrong color, but since I was testing the item as a Vine reviewer, I will overlook that. The good: This swimsuit is SOOO comfortable. It's super soft fabric and probably the most comfortable swimsuit I've had. The bad: The top does not offer any support even with the underwire. For those that have a smaller chest size or don't need extra lift, that's fine, but I wear a 32DD, so I like to feel like the top is actually doing something. I felt like this one was just sort of laying on my chest and the top slipped up while I was just trying it out in my house, so for that reason I would hesitate to wear it in a public place. The fit was true to size, but not flattering for my body. It might look great on some people, but the high waisted look was not doing it for me. It made me look boxy and also heavier than I actually am. I have attached a photo to illustrate. For a point of reference, I'm 5'6" and 115 pounds, and my measurements are 36-25-34. This particular item did not do me any favors, but again, different body types might rock it. I will say again, this swimsuit fabric feels VERY nice and super comfy. I would wear it to lie out by the pool at my house where it's less of a concern if the top malfunctions.

I’m 5’2 and about 115 lbs for reference. This bikini got me more compliments than others that cost triple. This style is reversible so it’s like two bikinis in one. It’s flattering as it’s high waisted to give you some tummy coverage and cinches in your waist. I love high waisted bikinis, but many can be too much material and look a bit frumpy. This is high cut on the leg and a thong so it’s not too much coverage, but covers where I like coverage most. Surprisingly the top gives decent support even though I’m a D cup. I tend to carry weight in my middle so I hate when material cuts into giving me a muffin top, but I just kinda stretched out the waistband with my hands before wearing and that helped the waistband become more stretchy to avoid the fat hanging over. Even my 15 year old daughter and 14 year old niece complimented me for the on trend pattern with the little red cherries, super cute! I wound up buying this pattern after I had previously ordered the black and white reversible style which is more classic than this more trendy style. This style has fast shipping and many options for different colors. I would pick your favorite color and if it fits well you may be like me and want to order in another pattern.

When I ordered this suit, it was priced at $19.99. What a steal for this cute little swimsuit. QUALITY: Surprisingly high. All the seams were finished well, and the colors and print looked just like the picture. SIZING: I'm 5'5" and 150 lbs and normally order a Medium. The medium bottoms fit perfectly. The top fit fine once it was on, but boy, it was a struggle to get it over my head and down my torso because it was so tight and didn't stretch much at all. Once I got everything in place, it was fine, though still a little tight around my upper torso. TUMMY CONTROL: I didn't really detect any tummy control, but the bottoms fit nicely and were high waisted. They didn't constrict at all, which I appreciate. STYLE: This is a flattering style and I'm looking forward to mixing and matching the pieces with a black bikini I already own! Very versatile pieces. NOT A MODEL: To state the obvious, I'm not a model and I didn't relish posting photos of myself in a swimsuit, but I thought it was important to see this suit on a real body. I hope my sacrifice was !

I tried Axesea's front zip suit and the zipper was too short to get in and out of the suit, a shame since their suits are a little more compression and have flattering ruching. (I bought a back zip one which is easy off but beastly to get on). I also have a Daci rashguard 2 piece, nice quality, but their 1 piece was too short in the torso. But this SELINK has some things going for it-- easy on and off, not huge boob pads that make you look like a 50's movie starlet. Excuse the odd poses,( and messy bathroom lol) but I was moving around seeing if the suit would stay put since I swim laps-- and it does( I also had underwear on under it, as I do for trying on suits). The aqua/black pattern is very flattering, even if the suit is not as snatched in as some other brands. I am not young, but it holds in older skin well enough! I got a medium, because other reviews said it runs large, and you want it smaller because it has a little give in the water. I am 5'9, 145 lb 36-28-37 w big ribcage and not much boobs, hips or belly. If you have a large chest or tush you might try an L, as well as M, at my height. I was really shocked this suit was long enough, I usually buy long torso suits. It does cut up a little bit on my bum, but without undies under and when it's wet I expect it will cover the cheeks. Overall very pleased with it. Can't attest to it's salt water capabilities, as bought it past my swim season, but price is good and it keeps sun of my arms and chest. Since the material is thin, I'd treat it delicately, no dragging your bum on the side of the pool, etc.

This swimsuit's quality and construction would pass for much more expensive brands. It's a very flattering design as well, with just enough coverage to look modest but not dowdy. The fabric is good quality with a nice, firm stretch and nicely done stitching. The back straps are thick enough to provide support and are adjustable in length. This suit looks and feels like some more expensive ones I've bought in the past for twice (or thrice!) the price. As far as sizing goes, I ordered a medium and it's a wonderful fit even with my 36DD bust size. The only (minor, tiny) issue is that the color of the pink sash is very similar to PeptoBismol in person (I expected a little more subdued shade of pink based on the marketing pics) but it works well nonethelesd in contrast with the olive green hue of the rest of the suit. As a bonus, the sash is not permanently attached so you can choose to wear it with the sash for a glam pool party look, or without it for a more active day at the beach. Love it and highly recommend it!

***Sorry in advance for the jumbled up photos! I purchased 2 swimsuit tops and 2 swimsuits bottoms from this company to compare so I figured I'd upload photos off all them for others to see some of the different tops and bottoms they have! 😊 Very impressed by the quality of this swimsuit top! I actually ended up buying 2 different tops and 2 bottoms from this company (I included photos of all for reference) and can honestly say that they are phenomenal quality and an exceptional deal. My only recommendation with the tops would be to SIZE UP. I'm a 34D (post baby) and after reading a few reviews I decided to buy the medium. It was cute but definitely too small (not in the amazing cleavage kind of way but the physically not covering enough kind of way 😂). I'm sure a size large would have been perfect though! If you're in the 34C range (probably B range too!) I'd definitely recommend the medium. Details about the swimsuit top: - Material is 80% Polyamide, 20% Spandex - Imported (Made in China) - Instructions on the tag say hand wash only, do not bleach, do not tumble dry, do not iron, do not dry clean, wash with similar colors and line dry! - There are small, removable padding in this swimsuit top which is nice because you can fully remove them or swap them for something more supportive (the ones it comes with are much better than quality than regular swimsuit tops however!) - Features adorable twisty/ cutout design to show off your girls - Shoulder straps are fully adjustable for the perfect fit - Adjustable criss-cross ties at back (the back of this swimsuit top is all around adorable) - Supportive yet comfortable - Great flexibility and elasticity so you can move around without worrying about whether or not your swimsuit is staying in place! Overall, this swimsuit is a smoking deal at $19.99 (especially compared to the $59.99 price tags!!) The quality is very impressive and the twisty, cutout design is absolutely adorable. The swimsuit was comfortable on but again, my only recommendation would be to size up with this one!

Wash the black first before wearing or the dye will bleed onto your skin and stain it black lol.

That is always the case for me, and was expected. If you have a larger backside, you will know what I mean. It is fine though. I love how the skirt doesn’t really flare out, but it’s A-line enough to be flattering. It fits pretty true to the pictures shown. I think this would be flattering on both thick and thin figures of all ages. I am a 30 year old mom and I don’t feel like it is frumpy like some swim skirts. This looks good with both tankinis and bikinis. I will wear it with both depending on the coverage I am looking for. PS- ignore the awful sunburn I have lol

It does have adjustable straps though and the slides and rings are made of metal, not cheap plastic. The criss-cross design should help slenderize your stomach area and accentuate your waist. I wasn't sure what to expect from this MOOSLOVER Slimming Tummy Control Two-Piece Swimsuit but let's just say I was pleasantly surprised! For reference I'm 5'6" 140 pounds, and kinda hippy with not much up top. I've always had trouble finding a suit that fits all areas -- too tight here, too loose there, not quite right. But this 2 piece suit works because each piece hugs the body in all the right places. The size M fits great, it’s true to size and flattering. The twist V-neck top is a perfect blend of sexy and modest, giving just the right amount of lift with the removable padding. And those adjustable straps are great for getting the fit just right. The ruched waist gives a slimming effect and the high-waisted bottom is not only trendy but also provides great coverage while still being stylish. The fabric is soft, stretchy, and comfortable. Did I mention how good I feel in it? So if you're looking for a swimsuit that looks good and makes you feel good, try this one. Whether you're sipping a mocktail by the pool or are on the back of a jet ski, it has you covered (literally!). It’s functional, cute, and sexy. Don't hesitate, this one is a winner.

I’m 5’6” and about 125lbs, 34B, 26” waist, 37” hips. I’m normally a size small, but my spidy-sense told me to order the Medium and I’m glad I did. I have a really long torso for my height, and I wanted to make sure the bottom would still be high-cut on me, which thankfully it is. I would say the quality of this suit is on the low side of mediocre, the fabric is nice enough, it’s sewn together well. But you get what you pay for. I’m mostly happy with the fit, although I wish the fabric was a little more forgiving on the sides of the hips, some of that is a “me problem” I have squishy skin and I’m 42. I also wish they would have patterned a seam on the back side of the bottoms, so it more elegantly fit the rear, as opposed to wedging & bunching. Because let’s be honest, a suit cut like this isn’t staying-put on anyone’s backside without double sided tape. The crisscross on the front looks great, but no mater how I tied it (I tried several ways) the crisscross just really dug into my sternum and felt uncomfortable. You’ll note that I have some photos showing it worn un-crossed. I would give it a 7 out of 10. Really fire Vampirella vibes. But a little uncomfortable and totally runs small.

I've never ordered a swimsuit from Amazon, so I was very skeptical this would fit me. I am 5'2" with a 32 inch waist and a muffin top. I would definitely say it was true to size, but if you're curvy it might be a little small in the chest area.

If you are like me and you think ordering a bikini online when you are in your mid-50s is a CRAZY IDEA, that's what I thought. But, I took a chance with this one and it paid off. Is it perfect? No. But am I so glad I ordered it? Yes! Here's the deal: First off, the last thing I want to do it post a picture of me in a bikini in my review, but I thought it was important to see the suit on a real body! So, you're welcome. I'm 5'5", 150 lbs and normally order a Medium. The high waisted bottoms are amazing and I don't know why I wasn't wearing these earlier. They fit perfectly, don't bunch, and aren't tight. I wouldn't say there is any "tummy control" to speak of...they just fit normal. The halter top has a cute ruffle and would probably look better on someone with larger boobs, but that's the way it goes sometimes. the top ties in back at the neck AND around your ribs. (no latch closure like you might expect) Overall, this is a cute style, fits true, and I'm looking forward to mixing and matching the top and bottom with other suits I owe. WIN!!!!

Lots of adjustability with the three straps in the back. Love the shirred top and built-in bra cups. The top half fits me great, but alas not the bottom. There is an excess of fabric in the crotch area and the leg openings don’t seem to have much elastic and are too loose for me. Perhaps it’s because I’m 5’1” tall or it’s simply the wrong size for me. I normally wear a size 10 and a Medium which is what this suit is. We all have different bodies and what works for one doesn’t necessarily work for someone else. Maybe it would be a better fit for someone with a longer torso than me. Also, it is described as a tummy control suit. I’ve had slimming swimsuits before and this one is nothing like them. It has a double layer of fabric in the abdominal area, but I wouldn’t call it a control panel. Overall, I think it’s a nice swim suit and If you can wear it, it’s an excellent value.

Right off the bat, I will say my girlfriend and I had high hopes for this. We loved the retro look and style of it in the pictures, so we ordered it. It came faster than almost any product we've ordered. My gf is 5'6 and 140 lbs. Not necessarily overweight but there is a small 40 year year old 'thicker in the middle' thing happening, although she hits the bike hard after work. The top was fantastic. She wears a Medium in most swimsuits, so we ordered a Medium. The top was incredibly cute and fit her 34C chest nicely. The bottom was horrible and looked like a Sumo diaper. While her tummy isn't flat, she doesn't have a big belly yet these bottoms really made her middle look wide and barrel shaped. Going up a size wouldn't be the answer ... it was a combination of the fit and her body shape. We've decided to throw out the bottoms, keep the top and pair it with a solid black standard bikini bottom. For teens and very svelte body types, it might work for you. I'd imagine XS or S sizes would look okay, especially if you have a long torso with a thin waist/stomach. For average sized girls that don't have a perfectly flat tummy, it's probably going to look strange. We ordered the black and white gingham matching top & bottom set. It looks somewhat like the pictures, except the pattern is a little lighter grey and looks printed on up close. If it fit right, it would absolutely be retro cute ... and the top really is nice when paired with a different style bottom. Overall, my recommendation is to order true to size and if you are a little pair shaped, don't attempt this. It looked so bad, my gf didn't want to post a picture of her wearing it. I'd recommend smaller body styles wear this best ... however, the suit has a fantastic retro 1940s or 1950s style which is very hard to find. I hope my honest description and pictures help you make an informed shopping decision.

I didn't expect to love this surf suit as much as I do, but it is AWESOME! I hate constantly putting on sunscreen, so it really helps with that. It's super comfortable everywhere on my body. I'm 5'2", 34DDD cup, 124 pounds, very evenly proportioned, and bought a size medium. I might have been able to buy a size small, but didn't want it to be tight fitting for athletic activities. I was worried that the built in shelf bra wouldn't support me but it does, and I'm no spring chicken. I took the removable cup pads out and go without them. I'm older so I appreciate the extra coverage on my upper arms, thighs, and tummy. I'm out there to have fun, no worry about my body.I spent 3 weeks wearing it daily on the beach hiking, kayaking, SUP, swimming, jetskiing, tubing, etc. on Lake Superior (pretty rough environment). It was super convenient for longs hikes that had swimming opportunities; I wore it with triathlon swim sneakers. So nice to go all day long with just a backpack and no other clothes! I thought it would look weird in a non-surfing environment, but it didn't; women actually asked where they could get one. I wear it all day on the dog beach in southern California as well. It's funny, when I wear it now, I think I understand why they wear those one-piece sleek space suits in sci-fi movies, they're totally comfortable and make your feel good, ha ha ha.I don't know how it would hold up in chlorinated water, but most suits don't do well in chlorine and I wear a normal swimsuit in the pool anyway. I am careful with washing it because I want it to last so I put it in a lingerie bag in the washing machine, and hang to dry. I'm most likely going to buy other versions of this same suit in the different fabrics from this same company so I have some variety. I have several other surf suits from different companies, but this one is my favorite.

I have yet to find after 26 years a suit I am comfortable in. It covers up the belly perfectly while still giving shape, the material isn’t like HIGH quality, but it feels really nice on. You can make it regular or high cut along with the butt cheeks being more full coverage to cheekey (to depending the size) thong like. I do not feel like I’m wearing a diaper. I hate one pieces because of that, but I hate feeling chunky in a “high waisted bikini” that cuts me off in the wrong spots. For reference I am 5’3” and 175 pounds some of it is muscle. I wear a 36c and a 8/10 for pants. I ordered a medium because I like feeling snatched in things and the measurements for the large weren’t too far off from the medium. I always go smaller to feel secure as my tummy is a little flabby and my boobs are not lifted in the slightest and they don’t feel like they are going anywhere. Also for spaghetti straps they don’t feel cheap like other suits I have tried where the seam will snap. They have a clasp for adjusting which I like and so far they haven’t dug in. Overall I would give it an 9/10 and 4 starts as I don’t give full 10’s unless it checks every single box, plus I haven’t worn it in water etc yet. This is not a paid review, just a girl actually happy with Amazon for once.

Reviewed by spouse: This is my new favorite rash guard. I have many as I’ve had two bouts with skin cancer so always want to be fully protected when I’m in the water. This one has a little mock neck that actually covers up the back of your neck a little bit better than just a crew neck and then with also wearing a hat you would be completely on the water. It’s very long…I would almost call this a sunscreen dress so it does cover the top of your legs as well when you’re walking into the water, but it can be synched up on both sides and becomes more of a shirt. For sizing reference, I am wearing a medium and that was the perfect size for me…I'm 5’3, 136 and 34c. The material is a good weight…not too thick where it would be hot, but not flimsy either. It also has thumb holes, so the material can go over your hands for protection as well…very important.

So, I have a chooks one piece, and this bikini from Amazon is a knock off of it. I will say they are very similar (have pictures that compare.) The chooks is better quality material. However, here’s what I like more about the Amazon one. The bottom is a little wide and less of a long, so you don’t have to worry about your lady area peeping out. I also like that it has removable padding. The color is lighter than I expected, but that’s okay. I loveeee the fact the straps are adjustable. The chooks suit you need bigger boobs to fill it out, and I’m only a 34B. SO, the adjustable straps make it fit better for me in the bust. Things I don’t like: I have a muscular build. With that being said, the area by my thighs is a little tight, but not too uncomfortable. Also, the pads are small and move around. Making your chest look odd, if they go down or up too far. It’s a cute suit for the price though.

This is a really pretty swim top. It has decent support from the stretchy material and the adjustable criss-cross straps in the back. It’s not for larger than a C cup breast. All I can say is that I'm excited about this swim set! I've never been into swim shorts or high waisted options for swim but after 2 kids and not getting back to a size 2, I appreciate the extra coverage. I love that the swim top itself is sporty, the straps are super cute with the criss cross over my tattoo. And did I mention how amazing the coverage is!! I ordered a medium and took a risk considering my chest size (augmented) 34DD-ish maybe a bit bigger with the weight. I'm about 155 pounds and only 5' 2". But wow! The top fits with the perfect amount of support, I was feeling like I was wearing string bikinis in ever style of "supportive" suit in the stores. So this suit is a game changer. The tank top is a little loose fitting and scoops low showing the sporty swim top beneath but I'm okay with that. The mom tummy is covered and I feel comfortable around others while out swimming. Give it a try, you will be pleased. Ps Sorry about my messy storage room, we're working on building a storage closet.
